title: Debugging Basics
description: Learn to read errors, find bugs, and fix them systematically.
objectives: Identify common error types (SyntaxError, TypeError, NameError, IndexError)
Use print() to debug step by step
recap: Reading error messages carefully is the #1 debugging skill. Python tells you the error type, the line number, and often what went wrong. Print statements help you inspect values at runtime.
practicePrompts: Intentionally cause a TypeError and then fix it based on the error message.
Add print() statements to trace a bug in a loop that produces wrong output.
